description: Family letter from a son to his father, Abu al-Muna al-Muwaffaq. Two main family matters are discussed. First, the son states that his brother-in-law, a Rumi, has stolen the Ketubba (marriage contract) of his sister. The son had intended to bring the Rumi to the head of the police or to the rabbinical court, but he had already run away. Secondly, the writer asks his father to allow his brother, Abu Ishaq, to spend the holidays with him. (Information from Mediterranean Society, III, p. 114, and from Goitein's index cards)
description_he: מכתב משפחתי מבן לאביו אבו אל-מונא אל-מופק. שני עניינים עיקריים: גיסו ה-רומי גנב את הכתובה של אחותו וברח; ומבקש לאפשר לאחיו אבו יצחק לבלות את החגים עמו.
